title: Refactor sanitization middleware
body:
**Development & PR Process**
1. Follow MetaMask [Mobile Coding Standards](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1VJLwTRsUw_5EDq_o8d6sSbXUAYENLurkRitYO45eY5o/edit?usp=sharing)
2. Add `release-xx` label to identify the PR slated for a upcoming release (will be used in release discussion)
3. Add `needs-dev-review` label when work is completed
4. Add `needs-qa` label when dev review is completed
5. Add `QA Passed` label when QA has signed off

**Description**

Add sanitiziation RPC middleware to process requests just before they get sent into our internal network provider. This is meant to be a refactor with no functional changes; this middleware is identical to [the middleware already used today within `web3-provider-engine`](https://github.com/MetaMask/web3-provider-engine/blob/9ac79a46ddbe37612027b08e22f395fd7dfeef04/subproviders/sanitizer.js).

This is being added so that we can remove `web3-provider-engine` with fewer functional changes.

**Test Instructions**

This should have no functional impact. This middleware is expected to process any RPC request not intercepted earlier in the pipeline however (essentially any request that gets sent to the RPC endpoint). It should ignore most requests (as the unit tests demonstrate), but it will process any that have an object as the first parameter. Known examples are `eth_call`, `eth_estimateGas`, and `eth_getLogs`.

To manually test this, you could try invoking these methods with parameters that the middleware should be expected to filter out. Or invoke them with parameters that are allowed, to confirm they remain unaffected. You could also try testing requests that we anticipate are unaffected. In all cases, we should see the same behavior before and after.

The test dapp has a new "Request" page that might be useful in testing this. See the README Usage section for details: https://github.com/MetaMask/test-dapp

**Issue**

Closes #5846

**Checklist**

* [x] There is a related GitHub issue
* [x] Tests are included if applicable
* [x] Any added code is fully documented

resolved_issues.title: Refactor: migrate sanitization middleware from `web3-provider-engine` to mobile
resolved_issues.body:
### **Description**

The package `web3-provider-engine` is used internally by the network controller (`@metamask/network-controller`). We are preparing to replace it. In preparation for this change, we are migrating some middleware functionality from `web3-provider-engine` into mobile to prevent this replacement from having any functional impact on the dapp API.

This issue is for the santization middleware, which formats the parameters of any request that looks similar to `eth_sendTransaction`.

### **Technical Details**

- Migrate the sanitization middleware from `web3-provider-engine` to mobile's `RPCMethodMiddleware` module.
- Ensure this will result in no functional changes
  - Ensure that the order of execution is identical, or that any differences will not affect the results
  - Ensure that by introducing a second copy of this santization step, we will not be affecting the results (if we find this to be non-idempotent, we can remove the `web3-provider-engine` santitization step using a patch)

### **Acceptance Criteria**

-  We are confident there are no functional changes
- We have ensured that the removal of `web3-provider-engine` will not change how RPC methods are sanitized

### **References**

This helps to unblock https://github.com/MetaMask/core/issues/970

title: Fix/primary currency fiat insufficient funds error
body: **Description** This PR adds the following changes to Amount screen: - Inserted amount is now validated correctly when Fiat is selected as primary currency in Settings; - It should now provide converted Fiat values to all tokens that have conversion rate available, not only for mainnet; - Improve safety while converting `number` to `BN`; - Fix inconsistent decimal cases between native tokens and ERC-20 tokens when converted to Fiat; **What to test** - ***Case 1 - Insufficient funds fix*** - Switch to "Binance Smart Chain Mainet" - Switch to "Fiat" as primary currency under "Settings > General" - Go to Send view - Send a value under your balance, it should proceed. - ***Case 2 - Insufficient funds fix*** - Switch to "Binance Smart Chain Mainet" - Switch to "Fiat" as primary currency under "Settings > General" - Go to Send view - Send a value above your balance, it should error "Insufficient funds". - ***Case 3 - Convert FIAT value for tokens (non-ETH)*** - Switch to "Binance Smart Chain Mainet" (other networks can also be tested) - Switch to "Fiat" as primary currency under "Settings > General" - Go to send view - Insert FIAT value - It should show converted BNB value Note: To validate correct conversion values please compare it with [cryptocompare](https://www.cryptocompare.com/) ***Screenshot*** **Checklist** * [x] There is a related GitHub issue * [x] Tests are included if applicable * [ ] Any added code is fully documented **Issue** Resolves #3983

resolved_issues.title: When primary currency is fiat, “Insufficient funds” warning/error message is shown
resolved_issues.body:
**Description**
When the primary currency is switched to fiat, “Insufficient funds” warning/error message is shown when trying to send funds out
- The screen then just hangs, and doesn’t let user proceed
- Reproduced consistently on BSC, Avalanche, Goerli (tbd whether this is an exhaustive list of networks)
- Doesn't break the core flow but doesn't allow user to send unless user change that fiat setting
- Not reproduced consistently: the “Next” button is lazy to load, takes >30 secs
- No fund loss
- Workaround
-- App doesn't crash and UI is still intact.  Workaround is to switch back to native in settings
- Hypothesis that needs investigating
-- looks like when you switch to fiat it still looking at the native balance, not the fiat balance

**Screenshots**
- On iOS production as old as v4.0.1: http://recordit.co/0SU7AJctIS
- On Android production as old as v3.1.0: http://recordit.co/TTW0CWCmqt

**To Reproduce**
1. Set primary currency to Fiat in general settings, 
2. navigate to send funds screen, 
3. set any fiat value, 
4. Present with “insufficient funds” warning regardless of balance in account
5. Some team members experienced the "Next" button hanging / not responsive; others didn't experience this. To be investigated


**Expected behavior**
When user switches to fiat, the "Insufficient funds" msg do not show up

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: iOS, Android

Sev1
https://consensys.slack.com/archives/CBW7S9FSN/p1648234626465419?thread_ts=1648233294.613979&cid=CBW7S9FSN

resolved_issues.title: Immediately resolves name as soon as I enter ".eth", but this excludes the ability to enter many names
resolved_issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When entering an ENS name into a Recipient field, as soon as I enter a possible ENS suffix, e.g. ".eth" or ".xyz", the app immediately resolves the name and doesn't let me keep typing. This is a problem because subdomains exist in ENS. E.g. the name evan.ethereum.eth exists, but when I try to type it in as soon as I get to "evan.eth..." it resolves the name and doesn't let me keep typing. This would also be a problem if I tried to send to "brantly.xyz.eth", the app cuts me off as soon as i typed in "brantly.xyz", even though "brantly.xyz.eth" and "brantly.xyz" are two different names with different owners and records.

**Screenshots**
_If applicable, add screenshots or links to help explain your problem_

**To Reproduce**
_Steps to reproduce the behavior_
1. Go to MetaMask Mobile on Android
2. Tap "Send"
3. Try to type in to the Recipient field the ENS name "evan.ethereum.eth"
4. You'll see that it cuts you off as soon as you type in "evan.eth..."

**Expected behavior**
You should be able to type in any name, and the app should never stop you from typing in more. As you type in, could present possible names below for you to select, but it shouldn't stop you from typing more.

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: Samsung Galaxy S21+
 - OS: latest Android
 - App Version: v2.4.0 (746)


-------------------------------------------------------------

- How critical is the impact of this bug on a user?
Could lead to lost funds

potentially everyone

yes

- Is this tech debt?
no, you just have a wrong implementation of ENS

resolved_issues.title: ENS confusables should not include the regular letter "m"
resolved_issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When an ENS name is entered and contains the letter "m", Metamask flags this (incorrectly in my opinion) as a confusable character. This also appears to happen to the digits "0" and "1".

**Screenshots**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/95138671/155265337-6a2785ab-e5de-446a-ade2-4ebc83a8c8d6.png)

**To Reproduce**
Enter any ENS name that contains "m", like temp.eth
Enter any ENS name that contains "0", like 0xbitcoin.eth
Enter any ENS name that contains "1", like 1bitcoin.eth

**Expected behavior**
Metamask should not display that "potential scam" warning when the name just has a letter m in it

In general, if a name is only comprised of "regular" characters [a-z0-9], the confusable warning message should not appear.

hints:
I see that this was pointed out in multiple places on the original feature, but was dismissed as "not a bug":

https://github.com/MetaMask/metamask-mobile/pull/2464#issuecomment-819799465



The same thing appears to happen for the digits "0" and 1" too. Maybe other characters too.

In general, the "confusable" message should not be shown for any character [a-z0-9].

resolved_issues.title: Txn Status Issue: The speed up and cancel CTA’s appear on the receiver ( the person who received the tokens) end.
resolved_issues.body: **Describe the bug** The speed up and cancel CTA’s appear on the receiver ( the person who received the tokens) end. Meaning a user sends some eth with a low gas fee to one of his accounts (lets call this the sender account). The sender account then speeds up the transaction. Upon doing so the transaction becomes confirmed. Fine. If you were to go to the receiver account then open up the transactions view, you will see a transaction with the speed up and cancel CTA’s. **Screenshots** **To Reproduce** 1. Go through the send flow to send some eth to one of your accounts 2. Before submitting your txn change the gas fee to low 3. Submit txn 4. Go to the transaction overview and speed up the txn 5. Wait until the txn is confirmed then switch to the account that you are receiving funds in 6. go to the transaction overview 7. notice the bug **Expected behavior** The receiver transaction overview should not contain any transaction that requires him/her to speed up the transaction. **Smartphone (please complete the following information):** - Device: [e.g. iPhone6] - OS: [e.g. iOS8.1] - App Version [e.g. 1.0.0] - find version number in app from Settings > About MetaMask ------------------------------------------------------------- _to be added after bug submission by internal support / PM_ **Severity** - How critical is the impact of this bug on a user? - Add stats if available on % of customers impacted - Is this visible to all users? - Is this tech debt?

resolved_issues.title: NFT Caching issue
resolved_issues.body:
### **Description**

- NFTs appearing into another accounts and networks, this happens because of an edge async issue between the CollectibleDetectionController, CollectiblesController, and current address and network. This can be verified by changing account/network right after starting the auto detection mechanism by refreshing the wallet view.
- This development also addresses the following breaking changes from the controllers version 21.0.1
  - https://github.com/MetaMask/controllers/pull/645
  - https://github.com/MetaMask/controllers/pull/636
  - https://github.com/MetaMask/controllers/pull/638
  - https://github.com/MetaMask/controllers/pull/655

### **Technical Details**

- Add a check to verify that the NFT is being added in the correct account and network that started the request (controllers layer).
- Add unit and integration testing to the new development and/or update existing tests.

### **Acceptance Criteria**

- NFTs should show up on the correct accounts and networks

### **References**

- [Dave Pazdan's Issue #3033](https://github.com/MetaMask/metamask-mobile/issues/3033)
- [Dan Finlay's Issue #1663](https://github.com/MetaMask/metamask-mobile/issues/1663)
- [Issue #3406](https://github.com/MetaMask/metamask-mobile/issues/3406)
